name: Monesterio and Calera de León via Vía de la Plata Loop
name_original: La Dehesa de Calera de León – running circular desde Monesterio por la Vía de la Plata
description: Jog the 8.5-mile Monesterio and Calera de León via Vía de la Plata Loop, a moderate route through historic dehesas and scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Monesterio traverse the Sierra Morena foothills in Extremadura, offering varied terrain for runners. The region is characterized by the expansive Dehesa, featuring oak pastures with cork and holm oaks, often delineated by traditional stone walls. Runners can also find routes with elevation changes in areas like the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, providing panoramic views. The landscape includes gentle paths alongside the Cala River and tranquil lagoons nestled within the dehesa. Best jogging routes around Monesterio The most popular jogging route is Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop, an 8.5 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This route leads through dehesas and alongside the Cala River, offering a scenic experience. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto de Aguafría – running circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area, a difficult 7.4 miles (12.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, known for its natural beauty. Local runners also love the Monesterio and Calera de León via Vía de la Plata Loop, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the characteristic dehesa vegetation, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Monesterio is defined by expansive oak pastures, varied mountainous terrain, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate trails to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Monesterio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Monesterio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Monesterio?
A: There are over a dozen running routes around Monesterio, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult, traversing the unique landscapes of the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Monesterio?
A: The jogging trails in Monesterio primarily feature the distinctive &lt;em&gt;Dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape, characterized by expansive oak pastures with cork and holm oaks. You'll also encounter varied mountainous terrain in areas like the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, offering elevation changes and panoramic views, as well as gentle paths alongside the Cala River.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991760630/alto-de-aguafria-running-circular-from-the-cruz-del-puerto-recreational-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Aguafría – running circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12 km path with significant elevation gain through the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, known for its natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular running paths in the Monesterio area?
A: Many of the running paths around Monesterio are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992964564/monesterio-and-calera-de-leon-via-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monesterio and Calera de León via Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7 km route that takes you through the characteristic dehesa vegetation in a loop.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options near Monesterio?
A: While many routes offer moderate difficulty, shorter loops can be suitable for families. For a more accessible option, consider a portion of the historic Vía de la Plata, which offers gentle routes through the dehesa. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it suits your family's needs.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see while running?
A: While running, you can experience the expansive &lt;em&gt;Dehesa&lt;/em&gt; with its ancient oak trees and traditional stone walls. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, follow the scenic Cala River. You might also spot a tranquil lagoon nestled within the dehesa, providing a peaceful resting point. For specific natural monuments, explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles near the running routes?
A: While the focus of the running routes is natural beauty, Monesterio is rich in history. You can find historical attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which can be incorporated into longer walks or exploration after your run. The ancient Vía de la Plata also passes through the area, offering a sense of historical significance.

Q: What is the best time of year for outdoor activities like running in Monesterio?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for outdoor activities. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes, especially the dehesa, are at their most vibrant, providing a pleasant environment for running.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Monesterio?
A: The running routes in Monesterio are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from the serene oak pastures of the dehesa to the more challenging ascents in the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, and the scenic beauty along the Cala River.

Q: Are there shorter running loops suitable for a quick workout?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4 km route that can be completed in under an hour, offering a convenient option for a quick workout.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in Monesterio?
A: Running routes in Monesterio vary in duration depending on their length and difficulty. For example, a moderate 13.8 km route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 1 hour 29 minutes, while shorter loops can be completed in less than an hour.
tips: The Vía de la Plata, as it passes through Monesterio, especially in the La Dehesa area, offers one of the most beautiful and representative stretches of the Extremadura landscape: a Mediterranean pastureland, with holm oaks, cork oaks, and pastures where Iberian pigs, cows, and sheep graze.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate for jogging. While parts of the broader Vía de la Plata can be challenging, this specific loop is considered less strenuous, with gentle ascents and descents. It's well-suited for joggers looking for a manageable experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía de la Plata Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain. You'll start on some asphalt sections before transitioning to unpaved paths, dirt tracks, and old Roman roads. Be aware that some sections, particularly after rain, can become muddy. The path includes gentle ascents and descents, such as the climb towards Cerro del Coso near Calera de León.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and attractive landscapes. The route meanders through extensive dehesas—traditional Mediterranean woodlands with scattered oak trees, known for Iberian pig farming. You might also see cork oak forests. As you approach Calera de León, the scenery shifts, offering a contrast between cereal plains and mountainous areas, with panoramic views of Monesterio from higher points. Depending on the season, you might even spot fields of poppies.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural sites can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in history, being a section of the ancient Vía de la Plata - La Dehesa, originally a Roman road and later a Camino de Santiago pilgrimage path. You'll pass through the Comarca de Tentudía, an area known for its history and art. Notable landmarks include the impressive 13th-century Monastery of Santa María de Tentudía and the majestic Conventual Santiaguista in Calera de León.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the Monesterio trailhead are not provided, Monesterio is the starting point for this loop. You should be able to find parking within the town to begin your jog.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this route. While the Vía de la Plata can be challenging in summer due to heat, the milder temperatures of spring and autumn make for a more comfortable experience. You might also catch fields of poppies in spring.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog this loop. It's a public route, part of the Vía de la Plata.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, sharing about 42% of its path with these trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Both Monesterio and Calera de León are towns along the route, so you can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in both locations. These can be good spots for a break or to refuel.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a loop trail, meaning you will start and finish in the same location without having to retrace your steps.

waypoints.name: Vía de la Plata - La Dehesa
